ACT — Housing / building & development (current, from act.org.nz/infrastructure; ACT addresses housing supply through resource-management and building reform rather than a standalone housing page)

Values: Remove red tape, attract investment, respect property rights. Support decentralised decision-making, user-pays where appropriate, and private-sector involvement.

Achievements ACT cites relevant to housing: Opening the door to overseas building products — reforms allowing thousands of high-quality international construction materials to be used in New Zealand, bringing down costs for homes and buildings. Cutting red tape through RMA repeal — leading the charge to scrap and replace the Resource Management Act with a property-rights-based system that enables faster, more predictable development.

Next goals relevant to housing: Pass a property-rights-based resource management regime to replace the RMA — a rules-based framework where development can proceed quickly provided it does not harm others' property or the environment, so people can build without being bogged down in compliance. Enable private investment in major infrastructure through Public Private Partnerships. (ACT's approach to housing affordability is supply-side: deregulate land use and building, lower construction costs, let the market build more homes faster.)
